req.title: Wire transfer
req.statement: A customer shall initiate a wire transfer requiring two-factor authentication; transfers above $100,000 require manual review.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines three distinct behavioural zones: (1) a standard wire transfer with fresh 2FA that should be queued with HTTP 200/201, (2) a high-value transfer above $100,000 that triggers manual review with HTTP 202, and (3) a transfer attempted with a stale 2FA token that must be rejected with HTTP 401. Boundary Value Analysis drives the $100,000 threshold tests (at-boundary and one-below). Equivalence Partitioning covers the normal valid-transfer partition. State Transition Coverage addresses the 2FA-state machine (fresh vs stale). Negative Path covers the authentication failure. Decision Table reasoning underpins the combined amount+2FA state matrix. Together these heuristics give full condition/outcome coverage of R-FIN-006.

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_under_threshold_fresh_2fa_queued
preconditions: Customer account exists with sufficient balance
2FA was verified within the last 5 minutes
Transfer amount is $50,000 (well within the $100,000 threshold)
actions: Obtain a session token via <fixture: fresh_2fa_session>
POST /transfers/wire with amount=50000 and valid payload
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains status field equal to 'queued'
Response body contains compliance_check field indicating check started
trace: R-FIN-006: compliance check started expected outcome
R-FIN-006: 2FA verified in last 5 min precondition satisfied
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_wire_transfer_under_threshold_fresh_2fa_queued(fresh_2fa_session, api_base_url):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {fresh_2fa_session['token']}"}
    payload = {
        "amount": 50000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "beneficiary_account": "<test_beneficiary_account>",
        "reference": "R-FIN-006-EP-POS"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/transfers/wire",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "transfer_id" in body, "transfer_id missing from response"
    assert body.get("status") == "queued", f"Expected queued, got {body.get('status')}"
    assert "compliance_check" in body, "compliance_check field missing"
    assert body["compliance_check"].get("started") is True

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_stale_2fa_returns_401
actions: Obtain a stale session token via <fixture: stale_2fa_session>
POST /transfers/wire with amount=50000 and stale auth header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an error code indicating 2FA session expired
trace: R-FIN-006: 2FA precondition must be verified in last 5 min
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_wire_transfer_stale_2fa_returns_401(stale_2fa_session, api_base_url):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {stale_2fa_session['token']}"}
    payload = {
        "amount": 50000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "beneficiary_account": "<test_beneficiary_account>",
        "reference": "R-FIN-006-NEG-STALE"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/transfers/wire",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_code = body.get("error") or body.get("code", "")
    assert "2fa" in str(error_code).lower() or "auth" in str(error_code).lower(), \
        f"Error code does not indicate 2FA failure: {error_code}"
    assert "transfer_id" not in body, "transfer_id must not be present on 401"

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_at_100k_threshold_returns_202_manual_review
preconditions: Customer account exists with sufficient balance
2FA was verified within the last 5 minutes
Transfer amount is exactly $100,000 (on-boundary for manual review)
actions: Obtain a session token via <fixture: fresh_2fa_session>
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains status field equal to 'pending_manual_review'
Response body indicates compliance check started
trace: R-FIN-006: transfers above $100,000 require manual review (on-boundary at exactly $100,000)
R-FIN-006: error pathway 202 when manual review required
R-FIN-006: compliance check started expected outcome
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_wire_transfer_at_100k_threshold_returns_202_manual_review(fresh_2fa_session, api_base_url):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {fresh_2fa_session['token']}"}
    payload = {
        "amount": 100000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "beneficiary_account": "<test_beneficiary_account>",
        "reference": "R-FIN-006-BVA-100K"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/transfers/wire",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 202, f"Expected 202 for manual review, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "transfer_id" in body, "transfer_id missing"
    assert body.get("status") == "pending_manual_review", \
        f"Expected pending_manual_review, got {body.get('status')}"
    assert "compliance_check" in body
    assert body["compliance_check"].get("started") is True

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_just_below_100k_not_manual_review
preconditions: Customer account exists with sufficient balance
2FA was verified within the last 5 minutes
Transfer amount is $99,999.99 (one cent below the manual review threshold)
actions: Obtain a session token via <fixture: fresh_2fa_session>
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains status equal to 'queued'
No manual_review flag is set to true in the response
trace: R-FIN-006: transfers ABOVE $100,000 require manual review; $99,999.99 is strictly below
R-FIN-006: wire queued expected outcome for sub-threshold transfers
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_wire_transfer_just_below_100k_not_manual_review(fresh_2fa_session, api_base_url):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {fresh_2fa_session['token']}"}
    payload = {
        "amount": 99999.99,
        "currency": "USD",
        "beneficiary_account": "<test_beneficiary_account>",
        "reference": "R-FIN-006-BVA-BELOW"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/transfers/wire",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), \
        f"Expected 200/201 for sub-threshold, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "queued", \
        f"Expected queued, got {body.get('status')}"
    assert body.get("manual_review") is not True, \
        "manual_review must not be true below threshold"

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_2fa_state_transition_fresh_to_stale_mid_session
preconditions: Customer holds a session token that was valid at issuance
The 2FA timestamp on the token is manipulated to appear 6 minutes old via <fixture: expired_2fa_token_factory>
actions: Construct an auth token whose 2fa_verified_at is 6 minutes in the past using <expired_2fa_token_factory>
POST /transfers/wire with that token and amount=10000
expected: System transitions from 2FA-valid state to 2FA-stale state and rejects the request
Response includes error indicating re-authentication required
trace: R-FIN-006: 2FA verified in last 5 min precondition; state transition from fresh->stale causes 401
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_wire_transfer_2fa_state_transition_fresh_to_stale_mid_session(
    expired_2fa_token_factory, api_base_url
):
    stale_token = expired_2fa_token_factory(minutes_old=6)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {stale_token}"}
    payload = {
        "amount": 10000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "beneficiary_account": "<test_beneficiary_account>",
        "reference": "R-FIN-006-STC-STALE"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/transfers/wire",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401, \
        f"State transition to stale 2FA must yield 401, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error") or body.get("message"), \
        "Response must contain an error/message field"

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_decision_table_above_threshold_fresh_2fa
preconditions: Customer account exists with sufficient balance
actions: Obtain a session token via <fixture: fresh_2fa_session>
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-006: Decision table row: 2FA=fresh AND amount>100000 => 202 + manual review
R-FIN-006: transfers above $100,000 require manual review
R-FIN-006: compliance check started expected outcome
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_wire_transfer_decision_table_above_threshold_fresh_2fa(fresh_2fa_session, api_base_url):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {fresh_2fa_session['token']}"}
    payload = {
        "amount": 150000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "beneficiary_account": "<test_beneficiary_account>",
        "reference": "R-FIN-006-DT-ABOVE"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/transfers/wire",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 202, \
        f"Expected 202 for amount>100k with fresh 2FA, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "pending_manual_review", \
        f"Expected pending_manual_review, got {body.get('status')}"
    assert "transfer_id" in body
    assert body.get("compliance_check", {}).get("started") is True
